docs.title: Nova Analysis
docs.text: A personal intelligence system built on ZeroClaw.

docs.title: What this is
docs.text:
Nova Analysis is a toolkit that turns your AI agent into a working intelligence system:

  • Nova — monitors YouTube 24/7 and converts videos into business intelligence briefs
  • Second Brain T — turns any folder of documents into a searchable knowledge base
  • Telegram — command both from your phone, anywhere

docs.title: Who this is for
docs.text: Anyone who wants to own AI infrastructure, not just use AI tools.

docs.title: What you get
docs.text:
• A YouTube analyst that runs on your VPS and reports to your Telegram
  • A knowledge base built from your own documents (PDFs, notes, research)
  • One command to query everything from Telegram

docs.title: Get started
docs.text: → Full setup guide

docs.title: Core Purpose
docs.text:
• Extract high-signal business insights from digital content
  • Transform raw information into actionable intelligence
  • Specialize in YouTube content analysis for entrepreneurs

docs.title: Capabilities
docs.text:
• Rapid content deconstruction
  • Business model pattern recognition
  • Strategic insight extraction

docs.title: Operational Mandate
docs.text: Cut through noise. Deliver value. Nothing more.

docs.title: Nova Skill
docs.text:
Workflow: 1. Receive a YouTube URL. 2. Call apify-youtube__streamers--youtube-scraper once. 3. Extract the returned datasetId. 4. Call apify-youtube__get-actor-output once with that datasetId. 5. Analyze the returned data. 6. Stop and answer.

Rules: - Never call the same tool repeatedly unless the previous call clearly failed. - Normal maximum: 2 tool calls. - First line must be one of: - HIGH ROI - MEDIUM ROI - LOW ROI, skip it - Then bullet points only. - Max 300 words.

docs.title: Signal Over Noise
docs.text:
• Brevity is intelligence
  • Conclusions first
  • Zero tolerance for fluff

docs.title: Decision Framework
docs.text:
• If no clear value: output "Low ROI, skip it."
  • Always lead with the most critical insight
  • Compress complex ideas into laser-focused bullets

docs.title: Ethical Boundaries
docs.text:
• Never fabricate information
  • Always attribute insights
  • Prioritize actionable intelligence

docs.title: Structural Rules
docs.text:
• First line: ROI verdict (HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW)
  • Subsequent lines: Pure bullet points
  • Maximum 5 key insights
  • Under 300 total words

docs.title: Tone & Voice
docs.text:
• Financial investigative
  • Forensic detail
  • Zero emotional language
  • Pure strategic analysis

docs.title: Formatting Mandates
docs.text:
• Start with conclusive statement
  • Use concise, action-oriented language
  • Avoid qualifiers and hedging
  • Focus on quantifiable insights

docs.title: Core Focus
docs.text:
• Target: "Super Individual" business models
  • Priority: High-leverage, low-overhead strategies
  • Interest: Scalable personal entrepreneurship

docs.title: Communication Preferences
docs.text:
• Direct communication
  • Actionable insights
  • No academic or theoretical meandering

docs.title: Analytical Priorities
docs.text:
• Identify unique business model patterns
  • Extract monetization strategies
  • Highlight scalability potential

docs.title: Week 1 — VPS Setup
docs.text:
• Installed ZeroClaw on Hetzner Ubuntu 24.04
  • Fixed pip externally-managed-environment error with --break-system-packages
  • Connected Nova to Telegram via zeroclaw channel

docs.title: Week 4 — Portfolio
docs.text:
• Built MkDocs site
  • Deployed to GitHub Pages

docs.title: Nova — YouTube Intelligence Agent
docs.text: Nova is a Content Intelligence Officer that converts YouTube videos into business intelligence briefs.

docs.title: What Nova does
docs.text:
1. Receives a YouTube URL via Telegram
  2. Scrapes the transcript and metadata
  3. Outputs a structured brief — under 300 words

docs.title: Output format
docs.text:
Every brief starts with an ROI verdict, then bullet points only:

HIGH ROI
- Founder bootstrapped to $4M ARR with zero paid marketing
- Core mechanic: weekly email digest monetised via sponsorships
- Replicable model: audience first, product second
- Key insight: niche authority compounds faster than broad reach

docs.title: Configuration files
docs.text: File Purpose IDENTITY.md Who Nova is SOUL.md Core operating principles SKILL.md Workflow and tool calls STYLE.md Output format rules USER.md Your preferences and context

docs.title: Second Brain T — Knowledge Base Builder
docs.text: Second Brain T turns any folder of documents into a searchable knowledge base that your ZeroClaw agent can query from Telegram.

docs.title: What it does
docs.text:
Point it at any folder and it automatically builds:

  • Knowledge graph — visual map of how your files connect to each other
  • Wiki — one page per file, organised by topic with cross-links
  • Tiers — compressed summaries optimised for AI queries

docs.title: Supported file formats
docs.text:
• PDF — auto-converted
  • Word documents (.docx) — auto-converted
  • PowerPoint (.pptx) — auto-converted
  • Markdown notes
  • Plain text
  • Code files (Python, JavaScript, TypeScript)

docs.title: How to build your knowledge base
Step 1 — Prepare your files
docs.text:
Gather the documents you want to include. Examples:

  • Research papers
  • Course readings
  • Meeting notes
  • Project documentation
  • Personal notes

docs.title: Step 2 — Upload to your VPS
docs.text:
Open your local terminal and run:

scp -r /path/to/your/folder root@YOUR_VPS_IP:/root/.zeroclaw/workspace/knowledge/

Replace /path/to/your/folder with your actual folder path.

Mac tip: Right-click your folder, hold Option, click "Copy as Pathname" to get the path.

docs.title: Step 3 — Build the knowledge base
docs.text:
SSH into your VPS and run:

sbt build

This scans all your files, extracts topics and connections, and builds the output. Takes 1-2 minutes depending on folder size.

docs.title: Step 4 — Query from Telegram
docs.text:
Once built, message your ZeroClaw bot on Telegram:

Read full inventory:

use shell to run: cat /root/.zeroclaw/workspace/second-brain-t/output/tiers/index.md

Search for a keyword:

use shell to run: grep -ri "your topic" /root/.zeroclaw/workspace/second-brain-t/output/tiers/

Read a specific topic:

use shell to run: ls /root/.zeroclaw/workspace/second-brain-t/output/tiers/topic/

Rebuild after adding new files:

sbt build

docs.title: What you get after building
docs.text:
output/
  tiers/
    index.md          Full inventory of everything
    topic/            One file per topic cluster
    entity/           Deep context per document
  wiki/               Browse in Obsidian
  graph/
    report.md         Hub documents, gaps, connections
    graph.html        Interactive visual map

docs.title: Privacy
docs.text: Your files never leave your VPS. Second Brain T runs entirely locally — no cloud, no API calls, no data sharing.

docs.title: What you need
docs.text:
• A VPS (Hetzner, 2 vCPU / 4GB RAM, Ubuntu 22.04+) — ~$6/month
  • A Telegram bot token (free, via @BotFather)
  • An OpenRouter API key (openrouter.ai, $5 minimum)

docs.title: Step 1 — Get ZeroClaw running
docs.text: Follow the ZeroClaw official setup guide.

docs.title: Step 2 — Clone Nova Analysis
docs.text:
SSH into your VPS and run:

git clone https://github.com/itozija/nova-analysis.git /root/nova-analysis
bash /root/nova-analysis/setup.sh

docs.title: Step 3 — Add your files
docs.text:
Upload your documents to the knowledge folder:

scp -r /your/folder root@YOUR_VPS_IP:/root/.zeroclaw/workspace/knowledge/

Then build:

sbt build

docs.title: Step 4 — Connect Telegram
docs.text:
zeroclaw channel configure telegram
zeroclaw service restart

Done. Message your bot on Telegram.

docs.title: Adding Your Files
docs.text: This guide shows you how to upload documents to your knowledge base and rebuild it.

docs.title: Option 1 — Use the update script (recommended)
docs.text:
The easiest way. Run this in your terminal:

bash update-kb.sh

It will ask for your VPS IP address, then ask you to drag your folder in. It uploads everything and rebuilds automatically.

docs.title: Option 2 — Manual upload
docs.text:
Open your terminal and run:

scp -r /path/to/your/folder root@YOUR_VPS_IP:/root/.zeroclaw/workspace/knowledge/

Tip: To get your folder path on Mac, right-click the folder, hold Option, then click "Copy as Pathname".

Then SSH into your VPS and rebuild:

ssh root@YOUR_VPS_IP
sbt build

docs.title: Windows
docs.text:
Use WinSCP (free):

  1. Download and open WinSCP
  2. Click New Site
  3. Set protocol to SFTP
  4. Enter your VPS IP address
  5. Username: root
  6. Enter your VPS password
  7. Click Login
  8. Navigate to /root/.zeroclaw/workspace/knowledge/
  9. Drag your files or folders into that directory
  10. Open PowerShell, SSH into your VPS and run:
    ssh root@YOUR_VPS_IP
sbt build

docs.title: What files can I add?
docs.text: Format Notes PDF Auto-converted to markdown Word (.docx) Auto-converted to markdown PowerPoint (.pptx) Auto-converted to markdown Markdown (.md) Used directly Text (.txt) Used directly Code (.py, .js, .ts) AST parsed for structure

docs.title: After uploading
docs.text:
Once your files are uploaded and sbt build has run, ask your Telegram bot:

what's in my knowledge base?

Your new documents will be included in the response.

docs.title: Rebuilding
docs.text:
Every time you add new files, run sbt build again. It uses caching so only new or changed files are processed — rebuilds are fast.

You can also trigger a rebuild from Telegram:

rebuild my knowledge base

docs.title: Nova — YouTube Analysis
docs.text:
Send any YouTube URL to your Telegram bot and Nova returns a structured brief.

Example output:

HIGH ROI

Solo operator scaled newsletter to 50k subscribers in 8 months Monetisation: tiered sponsorships plus paid community at $97 per month Distribution: one long-form video repurposed into 12 short clips weekly Leverage point: systemised content pipeline runs on 4 hours per week Pattern: niche plus consistency plus one monetisation layer at a time

docs.title: Second Brain T — Knowledge Map
docs.text:
Run Second Brain T on any folder and get:

  • Documents processed and converted automatically
  • Topic clusters identified and linked
  • Everything queryable from Telegram in seconds
